purpose: Phase 2 Power Atlas data model for Matrix Reprogrammed. This file defines public-record atlas nodes, evidence classes, relationship-line types, source boundaries, and reader routes.

nodes.title: Central Intelligence Agency
nodes.summary: A public-record intelligence node for foreign intelligence, covert action history, oversight failure, declassified files, contractors, liaison networks, and black-budget architecture.
nodes.whyItMatters: The CIA lane anchors the Intelligence Dossiers and gives the atlas a disciplined route into declassified records, congressional oversight, covert-action history, and the limits of public visibility.
nodes.sourceBoundary: Use official agency pages, CIA Reading Room material, congressional investigations, court records, declassified files, and credible public-record histories. Do not treat speculation as confirmed agency conduct.

nodes.title: National Security Agency
nodes.summary: A signals-intelligence and cyber-power node for interception, metadata, encryption, cybersecurity, oversight disputes, and public-private communications infrastructure.
nodes.whyItMatters: The NSA node connects surveillance, cyber power, infrastructure, intelligence oversight, and the modern machine-state problem at the center of Matrix Reprogrammed.
nodes.sourceBoundary: Use official records, oversight material, declassified documents, public legal records, and credible reporting. Separate lawful mission, exposed programs, and speculative claims.

nodes.title: Five Eyes Intelligence Network
nodes.summary: A public-record alliance node connecting signals intelligence, liaison arrangements, surveillance architecture, and intelligence-sharing routes.
nodes.whyItMatters: This node lets the atlas connect CIA, NSA, GCHQ, MI6/SIS, allied agencies, surveillance history, and the internationalization of security power.
nodes.sourceBoundary: Use official public statements, parliamentary/congressional records, declassified material, oversight reports, and credible public-record histories.

nodes.title: Blackwater / Private Military Contracting
nodes.summary: A contractor node for outsourced war, Iraq-era records, private security, state dependence on private force, legal controversy, and accountability gaps.
nodes.whyItMatters: The Blackwater node connects the Intelligence Dossiers, Contractor Dossiers, war maps, procurement, legal records, and the privatization of state violence.
nodes.sourceBoundary: Use court records, official reports, congressional material, contract/procurement records, credible reporting, and the Contractor Dossiers. Avoid treating every contractor as criminal by association.

nodes.title: Epstein Public-Record Lane
nodes.summary: A public-record lane for court filings, exhibits, sworn claims, institutional failures, documented associations, flight-log references, and evidence-boundary analysis.
nodes.whyItMatters: This lane is a stress test for the entire site: names, contacts, allegations, court records, documented association, and proof must never be collapsed into the same category.
nodes.sourceBoundary: A name in a file, photo, address book, or flight record is not proof of wrongdoing. Separate defendants, convicted persons, alleged participants, witnesses, victims, employees, peripheral contacts, institutions, and disputed claims.

nodes.title: Cartel Power Systems
nodes.summary: A crime-network node for synthetic drugs, weapons, underground banking, ports, corruption, chemical precursors, laundering, violence, and state overlap.
nodes.whyItMatters: Cartels show how criminal power becomes logistics, finance, politics, chemistry, border pressure, prison influence, and transnational business architecture.
nodes.sourceBoundary: Use indictments, sanctions, court records, official law-enforcement statements, financial crime records, and credible reporting. Avoid operational detail that would enable crime.

nodes.title: Freemasonry / Symbolic Architecture
nodes.summary: A symbolic and public-record node for Freemasonry, ritual architecture, degrees, temple language, public membership claims, institutional history, and esoteric inheritance.
nodes.whyItMatters: This node connects D.O.G The Architect, SYMBOL, the Masonic degree series, monuments, media, money, architecture, and the symbolic layer of power.
nodes.sourceBoundary: Separate public records and verifiable membership claims from symbolic interpretation. A symbol is not proof of conspiracy. Commentary must stay labeled.

nodes.title: The War Machine
nodes.summary: A systems node for governments, contractors, intelligence agencies, weapons firms, sanctions, media narratives, NGOs, logistics, and human consequences of war.
nodes.whyItMatters: The war-machine lane lets the atlas connect public contracts, intelligence claims, civilian cost, migration, energy shock, supply chains, and reader routes into WWIII and Blackwater books.
nodes.sourceBoundary: Use official conflict data, procurement records, sanctions, parliamentary/congressional records, court material, and credible reporting. Do not present speculation as operational fact.

nodes.title: Medical Power / Compensation Systems
nodes.summary: A policy and compensation node for medical institutions, adverse-event reporting boundaries, vaccine compensation, pharma damages, public-health power, and evidence discipline.
nodes.whyItMatters: Medical power is one of the fastest ways for the archive to lose credibility if claims are not separated. This node enforces the boundary between reports, compensation, proven causation, and speculation.
nodes.sourceBoundary: Separate adverse-event reports, compensated claims, court findings, settlements, official statistics, and causation claims. A report is not proof of causation.

nodes.title: The Black File / Thirty-Three Systems
nodes.summary: The Black File is the gateway map that routes readers through intelligence, crime networks, contractors, secret societies, war, surveillance, psychology, human cost, and symbolic systems.
nodes.whyItMatters: This node turns attention into navigation. It is the central funnel between search traffic, AI answers, books, evidence policy, and the long-form archive.
nodes.sourceBoundary: Use it as a reader-routing device, not as proof of any individual claim. Every claim inside the route must inherit the evidence class of its source lane.
